comment: Launcher manifest (issue #226, scope item 2): the curated list of the bundled Toys. Each entry's `id` is the instrument document name under instruments/<id>.json; `title`/`blurb` are player-facing (blurbs adapted from the README rig table); `kind` is self-playing (sounds on load) or tap-to-play (surfaces trigger widgets); `order` sets grid position. The staging script (scripts/stage-assets.mjs) stages exactly these Toys + their transitive resources into public/. Default Toy is groovebox (order 1) — its assets are prefetched at splash so the first pick is instant.

toys.title: Groovebox
toys.blurb: A free-running 16-step drum machine — kick, snare, and hat synthesized from operators on a shared clock. Toggle steps, ride the tempo.

toys.title: Chord Player
toys.blurb: Tap and hold diatonic triad buttons (I–vii°). Chords re-spell live when you change key — a 12-voice pad.

toys.title: Strum Harp
toys.blurb: Drag to strum — the bar plucks a note each time it crosses a string. Strings stay in key through the tonal context.

toys.title: Euclidean Drums
toys.blurb: A self-playing drum machine with knobs to change beat density.
